tools.name: gate_check
tools.description: Check if a command is allowed by constraint gate. Returns ok/block/escalate.

tools.name: record_create_observation
tools.description: Record a constraint observation as a YAML file. Returns recorded status.

tools.name: record_update_observation
tools.description: Update an existing observation's status. Returns updated status.

tools.name: workflow_notify_artifact
tools.description: Notify that an artifact file has changed. Returns recommended MCP tools to call next based on registry triggers. Does NOT spawn processes.

tools.name: workflow_trigger
tools.description: Trigger a workflow by name. Returns the recommended MCP tool sequence. Does NOT spawn processes — the agent calls the tools explicitly.

tools.name: index_validate
tools.description: Validate YAML records against JSON schemas. Use AFTER writing records to verify correctness. Returns structured errors and warnings.

tools.name: index_update_claim
tools.description: Update a claim's verification status for a specific dimension. Use with apply=false first to preview, then apply=true to commit.

tools.name: index_extract
tools.description: Extract index entries from evidence markdown files. Idempotent — safe to call multiple times. Use after writing evidence to update the index.

tools.name: index_search
tools.description: Search index entries by capability, dimension, and status. Read-only query. Defaults to excluding candidate (unverified) entries unless explicitly requested.

tools.name: capability_generate
tools.description: Generate capability records from product surface adapters. Use dry_run=true first to check for drift, then dry_run=false to write.

tools.name: capability_list_probes
tools.description: List runtime probe files for a given stack. Read-only discovery.

tools.name: capability_list_verified
tools.description: List all verified claims and their supporting evidence. Read-only reporting tool. Defaults to excluding candidate (unverified) entries unless explicitly requested.

tools.name: workflow_classify_prompt
tools.description: Classifies a user prompt into one of 8 categories using keyword heuristics. Use BEFORE routing to specialized tools to determine intent. Returns category name, confidence score, and suggested tool names. Failure mode: empty prompt returns error.

tools.name: workflow_intake_orient
tools.description: Orients the agent by reading records/*/index, records/*/evidence, records/observations, and records/*/capabilities. Use AT THE START of an intake session to understand current record state. Returns structured overview: index entries, meta triggers, observations, capability files, and missing decisions. Failure mode: invalid category filter returns error.

tools.name: workflow_intake_plan
tools.description: Consumes orient output and produces an ordered verification plan. Use AFTER workflow_intake_orient to decide which records to read, which tools to invoke, and which questions to ask. Returns an array of ordered steps with verification type classification (static, import, runtime). Failure mode: missing orient_result or empty candidates returns blocked status.

tools.name: workflow_prepare_runtime_request
tools.description: Generates a structured approval request text and pre-conditions checklist for runtime commands. Use BEFORE requesting operator approval to run sandbox, container, or production commands. Returns an approval_request string and a pre_conditions checklist. This tool does NOT approve commands; always run check_gate before execution. Failure mode: missing required fields return error.

tools.name: workflow_convert_evidence
tools.description: Reads an evidence MD file, extracts the ## Findings section with [topic-tag] bullets, and maps them to an experiment YAML structure. Use WHEN migrating evidence markdown to structured experiment records. Supports dry_run (preview), migration (full rewrite), and structuring (new structure on existing). Returns experiment_yaml string, validation_errors, source_refs_linked, and status. Failure mode: missing or unreadable file returns error.

tools.name: workflow_candidate_to_experiment
tools.description: Maps a candidate assertion to an experiment draft. Reads the candidate assertion by ID, selects a dimension-specific template, and generates a ready-to-review experiment record. With auto_create=false (default), returns a draft for human review. With auto_create=true, creates the experiment record via record_create_experiment. The experiment always starts with status: draft and requires_human_approval: true.

tools.name: workflow_vendor_doc_assist
tools.description: Reads a vendor markdown document and suggests evidence frontmatter + ## Findings bullets. The tool does NOT write to records/ — suggestions are transient, human writes the final evidence file. Returns suggested_frontmatter, suggested_findings, cross_references, and notes. Use WHEN vendor docs need to be converted to structured evidence following the ## Findings convention.

tools.name: workflow_generate_prompt
tools.description: Reads a prompt blueprint file and extracts a skeleton section to produce a structured prompt object. Use WHEN the agent needs a ready-to-send prompt for a learning-loop task. Sanitizes context values before substitution to prevent indirect prompt injection. Returns prompt text, constraints, required_records, suggested_tools, budget_context, and approval_gates. Failure mode: unknown blueprint or missing skeleton returns error.

tools.name: workflow_intentional_skip
tools.description: Processes an intentional skip decision for an assertion. Use WHEN the operator or agent decides to bypass a specific assertion. Converts skipped knowledge into required loop artifacts so nothing disappears. Returns status (blocked, narrowed, accepted), records_required, blocked_work, allowed_work, and rationale. Failure mode: empty skip_reason returns blocked.

tools.name: workflow_verify_evidence
tools.description: Reads an evidence MD file, extracts code snippets and assertions, and classifies each assertion by execution class. Use BEFORE deciding which runtime checks are needed. Execution classes: symbol-exists, import-succeeds, method-callable, sample-output, full-runtime. Returns assertion_matrix, counts, skipped_snippets, required_approvals, and operator_confirmation_required. Failure mode: missing file returns error. IMPORTANT: Agent must NOT update validation_status to passed without explicit operator confirmation.

tools.name: workflow_external_decision
tools.description: Records an external decision and evaluates its acceptance against authority scope. Use WHEN a stakeholder or external system confirms a scope boundary. Seeds the decision into the loop while recording scope, basis, risks, and capability boundaries. Returns acceptance (partial/full/rejected), records_required, risks, capability_boundaries, and rationale. Failure mode: missing required fields return error.

tools.name: workflow_self_improvement
tools.description: Turns a self-improvement proposal into an experiment candidate with a canonical adoption path. Use WHEN the loop discovers a gap, heuristic failure, schema mismatch, or missing tool. Hard-test failures become evidence; canonical adoption always requires explicit operator decision approval. Returns experiment_candidate, decision_required, risks, next_steps, and canonical_adoption_path. Failure mode: unknown improvement_type returns error.

tools.name: workflow_report_phase_status
tools.description: Reports the status of a workflow phase. Use AFTER completing process steps to determine if the phase lifecycle is complete. Returns a status string and lifecycle_complete boolean. Failure mode: missing required fields return error.

tools.name: workflow_product_build
tools.description: Expands a user product request into structured assertions, risks, experiments, decisions, and required records. Use WHEN a product request arrives to decompose it into verifiable artifacts. References capability generation extension rules and meta-evidence self-improvement patterns. Returns assertions[], risks[], experiments[], decisions[], required_records[], and decision_coverage. Failure mode: empty request returns error.

tools.name: workflow_runtime_probe
tools.description: Plans a standalone feasibility probe script for a given stack and probe type. Use BEFORE requesting operator approval for runtime execution. References live-gate-template approval flow rules (env-var gate, fail-closed, operator decision record). Returns probe_plan, shared_env_requirements, per_stack_commands, and expected_outputs. Failure mode: empty stack returns error.

tools.name: record_create_decision
tools.description: Create a decision record YAML file. Use this before writing product-build plans. The record starts in draft status. For product/** writes, the write gate now requires a preflight marker (via mark_preflight_complete) instead of decision records. Decision records are still required for product-build plan.md files.

tools.name: record_update_decision
tools.description: Update an existing decision record by ID. Immutable fields (id, type, created_at) are preserved. source_refs is append-only: new refs are merged with existing, duplicates removed. Use to change status from draft to reviewed/approved, or update rationale/tradeoffs.

tools.name: record_create_experiment
tools.description: Create an experiment record YAML file. Experiments prove or disprove assertions. The flow is: draft assertions (decisions) → experiment to prove → evidence (derivative). Records start in draft status.

tools.name: record_update_experiment
tools.description: Update an existing experiment record by ID. Immutable fields (id, type, created_at) are preserved. source_refs is append-only: new refs are merged with existing, duplicates removed. Use to record results, change status, add observations, or update verification after experiment execution.

tools.name: record_create_risk
tools.description: Create a risk record YAML file. Risks document potential issues that may affect the project. Records start in draft status.

tools.name: record_update_risk
tools.description: Update an existing risk record by ID. Immutable fields (id, type, created_at) are preserved. Use to change status, severity, or add mitigation after analysis.

tools.name: gate_mark_preflight
tools.description: Mark the preflight checklist as completed for a given surface. This is the ONLY way to create a preflight marker — direct file writes to .loop-preflight-* are blocked by the write gate. After calling this tool, product/** writes for the surface are unlocked for 30 minutes. The marker file (.loop-preflight-<surface>) is stored in .claude/coordination/ (or .factory/coordination/ for Droid CLI) and has a 30-minute TTL.

tools.name: record_delete
tools.description: Soft-delete a record by moving it to a .deleted/ audit subdirectory (for decision, experiment, risk). Hard-delete evidence and claim files (no audit move, no status check). Requires operator_confirmation=true and a reason of at least 20 characters.

tools.name: index_validate_plans
tools.description: Validate all plan.md files for product-build tag compliance. Checks Phase 0 presence and decision records for each declared surface.

tools.name: budget_check
tools.description: Check resource budget status for a given system and resource. Returns budget, current usage, remaining, and stale status.

tools.name: meta_state_report
tools.description: Report a new meta-state finding to the agent-maintained registry. Status starts as reported with a 24h TTL until acked by an operator. Use this to internalize external references for `source_refs`. Optional but recommended: pass `evidence_code_ref` (code location) so the loop can hash and re-check it on demand via `meta_state_derive_status`. Markdown paths in `source_refs` are deprecated and will be rejected by `record_create_decision`.

tools.name: meta_state_list
tools.description: List meta-state registry entries. By default excludes terminal statuses (auto-resolved, expired, resolved). Runs auto-resolve and expiry checks before returning.

tools.name: meta_state_ack
tools.description: Acknowledge a reported meta-state entry, promoting it to active. This removes the 24h TTL.

tools.name: meta_state_resolve
tools.description: Mark a meta-state entry as resolved (terminal). Entry will be compacted after 7 days.

tools.name: meta_state_promote_rule
tools.description: Promote a meta-state finding to an active rule. Requires operator role. Writes a new entry_kind: 'rule' entry and updates the source finding's promoted_to_rule to the rule id string. Use preview:true to test pattern matches without activating.

tools.name: loop_describe
tools.description: Return the loop's current operational surface. **Recommended: call at session start to discover what the loop offers.** Supports tiered reads (hot/warm/cold/summary) to control context bloat.

tools.name: meta_state_sweep
tools.description: Walk the meta-state registry and propose (or apply) lifecycle transitions: expiry for reported entries past expires_at, auto-resolve for entries whose watched file was modified after creation. Dry-run by default. Operator-only (env: OPERATOR_MODE=1). CAS-safe via the version field. Use to keep the registry honest without manual per-entry work.

tools.name: meta_state_log_change
tools.description: Log a system change (schema, rule, tool, policy, surface, lifecycle, manifest) as a change-log entry in the meta-state registry. The entry is immutable, status=active, no TTL. Use supersedes to replace a prior change entry.

tools.name: meta_state_derive_status
tools.description: Derive the effective status of a meta-state entry by reading its stored references + the current filesystem state. Returns the locked shape: { id, raw_status, derived_status, derivation { kind, signals, checked_at, duration_ms }, drift, recommendation }. The agent decides what to do with the answer; this tool does NOT mutate entries.

tools.name: meta_state_check_grounding
tools.description: Check the grounding of a meta-state entry by computing its SHA-256 fingerprint and comparing to the stored value. Returns the locked shape: { id, raw_status, grounding { ... }, status, drift_kind, fingerprint_was_recorded }. On the first call, auto-records code_fingerprint when mechanism_check is true and the file exists. The agent decides what to do with drift; this tool does NOT auto-resolve entries.

tools.name: meta_state_refresh_fingerprint
tools.description: Refresh the SHA-256 fingerprint of a meta-state entry's evidence_code_ref. Use this when check_grounding returns status: 'drifted' with drift_kind: 'hash_mismatch' and you've decided the change is legitimate. Errors when mechanism_check is not true (nothing to refresh) or the file is missing. Returns { id, code_fingerprint, refreshed_at, status: 'refreshed' }.

tools.name: meta_state_query_drift
tools.description: Aggregate drift events across the meta-state registry. Joins SP1's deriveStatus + SP2's checkGrounding. Read-only: the agent decides what to do with the result.

tools.name: meta_state_propose_design
tools.description: Propose a new loop-design entry. Loop-designs are deferred designs with their own lifecycle (active -> inactive when shipped). Use this for designs that will create or modify rules, schemas, or tools. Mirrors meta_state_log_change's append-only semantics with the addition of proposed_design_for (forward: what the design ships) and addresses (backward: what findings the design responds to). Idempotent: same addresses + proposed_design_for set returns the existing entry id.

tools.name: meta_state_relationships
tools.description: Query the relationship graph for a single meta-state entry. Returns inbound, outbound, or both directions of cross-references (1-hop traversal only).

rules.description: Gate-enforced rule: rule-short-slug-for-risk-records. Pattern type=glob; pattern=records/**/risks/*.yaml. Originated from meta-260601T1353Z-sanitizeslug-in-record-writer-js-generates-a-kebab-case-slug (description: sanitizeSlug in record-writer.js generates a kebab-case slug...).

Gate-enforced rule: rule-no-new-artifact-types. Pattern type=regex; pattern=(propose|design|create)\s+(a|an|new|separate|own|the)?\s*(schema|artifact|directory|convention)|new\s+(schema|artifact|directory|convention). Originated from meta-260602T0000Z-escape-hatch-abuse-meta-taxonomy-proposal (description: Agent proposed a new artifact type (convention.schema.json, ...).

Gate-enforced rule: rule-project-skill-boundary. Pattern type=glob; pattern=.factory/skills/{use-mcp,find-skills}/**. Originated from meta-260602T1116Z-agent-inside-a-project-that-has-its-own-mcp-json-called-ck-u (description: Agent inside a project that has its own .mcp.json called ck:...).

active_findings.description: Closeout script (scripts/closeout-meta-evidence-migration.cjs) was not idempotent for the change-log append step. The script checked status for finding resolution (correctly idempotent) but unconditionally called writeEntry() for the change-log, generating a new timestamp-based ID every run. Result: two identical change-log entries (meta-260606T1433Z and meta-260606T1453Z) were created before the bug was discovered during code review. Fix: added a find() guard that checks for existing entry with same entry_kind + change_target + consolidates before calling writeEntry(). Cleanup script removed the duplicate. Pattern: all future direct-I/O scripts that append entries must implement idempotency guards (either deduplication check before write, or deterministic ID generation).

Gate-enforced rule: meta_state_resolve cannot resolve meta-260606T0443Z-mcp-tools-not-loaded-into-agent-tool-list unless the cold-session test (cold-session-discoverability.test.cjs#droid exec exposes mcp__learning_loop_mcp__* tools) has been run and its most recent evidence shows the gap is closed (no active finding with subtype=mcp-client-loading and session_id=test-cold-session-mcp-client-loading). The check is in checkResolutionEvidence in core/gate-logic.js. This rule design is captured in meta-260606T1531Z-cold-session-test-rule-deferred.

Context pollution: stale workaround language + audit-trail noise bias fresh readers toward "G8 is still broken." The G8 subcommand-class + message-body-fragmentation classes are BOTH fixed (P1 refined regex at meta-260606T0225Z + P2 quote-aware splitSegments at the change-log resolving meta-260606T0301Z, status=resolved). Empirical probe 2026-06-06 confirms: (a) `git commit -m "propose/design/create a new schema pattern"` returns decision=ok (P2); (b) `ck plan create --title foo --phases bar` returns decision=ok (P1); (c) legitimate triggers like `echo propose/design/create a new schema` still escalate (rule still enforcing correctly).

Three pollution sources:

1. AGENTS.md "use Create tool directly" + "git commit -F /tmp/msg.txt" workarounds predate both P1 and P2. They are now stale (proven by this probe) but still steer fresh agents away from canonical paths. A fresh agent reading AGENTS.md will use -F for safety, masking any future P1/P2 regression instead of catching it. Mitigation: replace the workaround paragraphs with a "verified working" note pointing at the regression probes.

2. Meta-state.jsonl audit trail: 4 superseded G8 finding entries (recurrences 1, 3, 4, 5), 3 change-logs, the meta-260606T0301Z finding, and the active rule entry combine into ~9 entries that all narrate the G8 fix story. The entries are correctly linked (consolidated_into, supersedes), but a fresh reader skimming meta_state_list will see a wall of "G8" and bias toward "G8 is open." Mitigation: out of scope for this finding — the audit trail is by design.

3. Self-applied workaround during mcp fix: I used `git commit -F /tmp/commit-msg-N.txt` for commits 1692b84 and a35074a. The probe shows this was unnecessary — inline `-m "..."` with trigger phrases would have passed. Applying the old workaround to a fixed bug is itself the pollution: future agents following my commit history will copy the -F pattern without questioning it. Mitigation: the next commit can use inline -m to reset the example, but no retroactive fix is needed.

Recommended follow-up (separate work):
- Update AGENTS.md § "G8 Subcommand Class" + "Create tool fallback" sections to mark them as historical workarounds superseded by P1+P2.
- Add a permanent regression probe in `tools/learning-loop-mcp/__tests__/` that runs the 5-case G8 matrix on every change to `core/gate-logic.js` or `promoted_to_rule.pattern` (locking in the fix so future regressions are caught).

Agent used direct file I/O (node -e scripts importing core/meta-state.js) for meta-state CRUD operations instead of the canonical MCP tools (meta_state_resolve, meta_state_log_change, meta_state_report, etc.). During Phase 0 of plan 260606-rule-loop-design-first-class, the agent bypassed the MCP tool layer for registry mutations. This is a procedural anti-pattern: the MCP tools provide validation, audit logging, and canonical semantics that direct I/O lacks.

Agent called meta_state_log_change MCP tool 5 times in succession due to apparent failure/uncertainty, each time appending a new change-log entry with identical change_target + reason but different timestamps. This created 5 duplicate entries (meta-260606T2059Z) in meta-state.jsonl before the agent realized the issue and cleaned them up. Root cause: the tool lacks a deduplication guard before writeEntry, and the agent's retry loop did not check for existing entries before re-invoking. Same pattern as meta-260606T2059Z-meta-state-jsonl closeout finding (which documented the same gap in a different script). The cleanup required manual Edit-tool deletion of 4 duplicate lines from meta-state.jsonl. This is the second recurrence of the duplicate-entry gap in the same session, proving the fix (manual cleanup) is not sustainable. A deterministic idempotency guard in meta_state_log_change or an agent-side dedup check before re-invocation is needed.

Deferred design (concrete adoption of loop-design-instruction-layer parent): add 7th hint to core/loop-introspect.js#DISCOVERABILITY_HINTS that teaches the agent the meta-vs-product-vs-in-flight surface split before answering "what's next?" style questions. Hint text: "For 'what's next?' questions, distinguish surfaces first: meta work lives in meta_state_list({ status: 'reported' }); product work lives in index_search on records/**/plans/; in-flight work lives in plans/<id>/phase-*.md. Don't query meta-state for product tasks." Trade-off decision: teaching hint over forced query (avoids prompt-attention competition + brittleness); teaching hint over new workflow_next_task tool (KISS + YAGNI — workflow_intake_orient already orients). Surface: core/loop-introspect.js (1 line in frozen array). Validation: cold-session test (rule-cold-session-test-must-pass-before-resolution) — agent asked "what's next?" must call BOTH meta_state_list AND index_search and tell the operator the surface split. Impact: 0 new schema, 0 new tool, ~50 tokens/agent-session. Status: deferred pending operator approval of parent loop-design-instruction-layer adoption. NOT shipped yet — this entry records the design decision so future agents see it in the registry without it being a done deal.

Top-level array and boolean parameters in MCP tool schemas get coerced to objects/strings on the wire, causing zod validation to reject valid input. Reproduced 2026-06-06 on meta_state_propose_design: passing proposed_design_for=['x'] or addresses=['y'] produces error 'expected array, received object' at path 'proposed_design_for'. Same pattern on meta_state_report: passing mechanism_check=false (boolean) produces 'expected boolean, received string'. Workaround used: drop the boolean field and accept partial registration. Root cause hypothesis: the MCP server's parameter parsing treats a single-element array as if the first element is an object key. Compare to meta_state_log_change which only takes arrays nested inside object fields (change_diff.added, applies_to.tools) — those work because the object wrapper absorbs the coercion. This is a different fault class from meta-260606T2106Z-agent-called-meta-state-log-change-mcp-too (which was idempotency, not wire format). Impact: agents cannot use meta_state_propose_design cleanly and fall back to meta_state_report (finding kind) for designs that should be loop-design kind, polluting the registry with the wrong entry_kind.

Dual-field schema risk: `evidence_code_ref` (top-level) vs `evidence.code_ref` (nested) causes active bug in `queryDrift` that skips SP2 grounding for 30 entries. The `finding` schema defines `evidence_code_ref` as top-level, but 30 entries in the registry carry only the nested `evidence.code_ref` form. The `change-log` schema defines `evidence.code_ref` as nested. Consumers `checkGrounding`, `deriveStatus`, and `refreshFingerprint` use a fallback chain `entry.evidence_code_ref ?? entry.evidence?.code_ref`, but `queryDrift` only checks `entry.evidence_code_ref` — skipping 30 entries from drift detection. The `meta-state-report-tool` writes both fields for backward compatibility; `meta-state-log-change-tool` writes only the nested form. No migration or normalization logic exists. `writeEntry` does not validate against Zod schemas, so invalid data shapes are accepted silently.

all_findings.description: The learning loop has no mechanism to surface the internalization rule to agents. When an agent creates a record with source_refs pointing to external artifacts (plans, docs, journals), the validation error only says source ref must stay under records/evidence. It does not explain the WHY or the internalization process. The agent natural response is to try legacy: as a workaround, bypassing the boundary instead of honoring it. The correct process is: extract relevant content from external artifact, create a meta evidence file under records/meta/evidence/, then reference the internal evidence via local:records/meta/evidence/. This rule is a feature not a bug, but it is not discoverable by agents. REVERTED 2026-06-02: the operator's earlier 'resolved' status (loop_describe exists, so the mechanism is shipped) was premature. The follow-up plan 260602-meta-state-lifecycle-tidy measured adoption = 0 in real sessions (G7, out of scope), and 260602-strict-mcp-call-rules is the mechanical fix via a Droid SessionStart hook. Until that plan ships and is verified in real sessions, the discoverability gap is real.
all_findings.resolution: Closed by plan 260606 Phase 2+3: discoverability_hints + cold-session test. Internalization rule surfaces in loop_describe warm tier. See plans/reports/brainstorm-260606-discoverability-and-meta-evidence-migration.md

all_findings.description: Agent could not discover the meta-state.jsonl registry or the meta_state_report MCP tool. The meta-state system is the correct destination for ephemeral agent findings, but it is not discoverable. The agent tried to create meta evidence files (records/meta/evidence/) which the write gate blocks, and did not know about meta_state_report as an alternative. This is a discoverability gap: the meta-state system is documented in AGENTS.md (budget-check rule references meta_state_report) but the agent had no way to find it without reading the specific file. REVERTED 2026-06-02: the operator's earlier 'resolved' status was premature. Until the 260602-strict-mcp-call-rules plan's SessionStart hook ships and adoption is verified in real sessions, the discoverability gap is real.
all_findings.resolution: Closed by plan 260606 Phase 2: loop_describe warm tier surfaces meta_state_report, meta_state_derive_status, meta_state_refresh_fingerprint, meta_state_log_change in discoverability_hints. See plans/reports/brainstorm-260606-discoverability-and-meta-evidence-migration.md

all_findings.description: Bash gate constraint matcher does not distinguish heredoc content from actual commands. The package-manager pattern matches "install" text inside string literals within a heredoc, causing false positive blocks. The stripMessageFlags function only strips message flags but does not handle heredoc content. The cat command with heredoc containing a description string with "install" triggered a package-manager block even though the actual command was creating a script file, not running a package manager.

use-mcp skill scripts under .factory/skills/use-mcp/scripts/ require node_modules setup but lack install guard. The skill is documented as primary MCP execution path but the scripts directory is not pre-installed. The agent had to run a package manager setup first, which was blocked by the bash gate. The skill should either ship with node_modules, provide a setup script, or document that dependency installation is a prerequisite step.
all_findings.resolution: use-mcp skill documentation updated; skill can be re-installed on demand. No persistent rule needed.

all_findings.description:
sanitizeSlug in record-writer.js generates a kebab-case slug from the full risk statement text. When the risk statement is long, the resulting slug is extremely long and causes ENAMETOOLONG when creating the risk record YAML file. The filename format is risk-meta-YYMMDDTmmZ-{slug}.yaml where the slug can exceed filesystem limits. The tool must either shorten the risk statement or provide an explicit slug parameter to avoid this.

RESOLVED 2026-06-05: rule-short-slug-for-risk-records promoted 2026-06-02T00:29:06Z. The rule (agent-enforced, glob pattern 'records/**/risks/*.yaml') is the resolution.

Agent proposed a new artifact type (convention.schema.json, 96 YAML files, conventions/ directory) and a three-tier taxonomy (meta-state → convention → decision) in plans/reports/brainstorm-260601-meta-taxonomy-redesign.md. This violates the philosophy: docs/ is an escape hatch, not a home for procedural knowledge; agents may not propose new artifact types. The correct approach is: curate the 96 assertions, encode critical ones in MCP tools/gate logic/prompts, delete the dead ones, and report gaps as meta-state entries. The report is now retained as a rejected anti-pattern exhibit. Prevention measures documented in plans/reports/brainstorm-260602-agent-docs-plans-default-pattern.md.

RESOLVED 2026-06-05: rule-no-new-artifact-types promoted 2026-06-01T22:00:13Z. The rule (gate-enforced, regex '(propose|design|create)\s+(a|an|new|separate|own|the)?\s*(schema|artifact|directory|convention)|new\s+(schema|artifact|directory|convention)') is the resolution.

PATTERN REFINED 2026-06-06: the regex was tightened to require a context qualifier (article + trigger noun) after the trigger verbs. New pattern: '(propose|design|create)\s+(a|an|new|separate|own|the)?\s*(schema|artifact|directory|convention)|new\s+(schema|artifact|directory|convention)'. The bare 'create' alternative was removed; 'create\s+(a|an|new|separate|own|the)?\s*(schema|artifact|directory|convention)' is the only 'create' branch. Closes the G8 subcommand-class false positive (7 recurrences).

First brainstorm report (brainstorm-260601-meta-taxonomy-redesign.md) formally rejected and superseded. The proposed solution — a new `convention` artifact type with `schemas/convention.schema.json`, 96 YAML files, and `conventions/` directory — violates the 'no new artifact types' philosophy and treats docs/plans as architecture rather than escape hatch. The second report (brainstorm-260602-agent-docs-plans-default-pattern.md) correctly identified the anti-pattern but proposed content-heavy prevention measures that don't use the loop's own machinery. Both are superseded by the architecture-level fix in brainstorm-260602-self-enforcing-loop-architecture.md, which makes meta-state.jsonl the rule registry via a `promoted_to_rule` field, a gate function that reads it, and a `loop_describe` MCP tool for self-description. First report's frontmatter has been updated to status: rejected; new report created as the superseding design.
all_findings.resolution: Source report has status: rejected. Entry served its audit purpose.

all_findings.description: Second brainstorm report (brainstorm-260602-agent-docs-plans-default-pattern.md) formally superseded. It correctly identified the anti-pattern (agents default to docs/plans as architecture rather than escape hatch) but proposed 6 content-heavy prevention measures — new philosophy rules, gate warnings, prompt injection, new meta-state category — that do not use the loop's own machinery. The anti-pattern analysis remains valid and is referenced by the superseding design. Superseded by brainstorm-260602-self-enforcing-loop-architecture.md, which makes meta-state.jsonl the rule registry via promoted_to_rule, a gate function, and a loop_describe MCP tool. Report's frontmatter has been updated to status: superseded; new section on restoration vs curation added to the superseding report.
all_findings.resolution: Source report has status: superseded. Entry served its audit purpose.

all_findings.description: Bash gate constraint matcher does not distinguish heredoc content from actual commands. The stripMessageFlags function (extracted from matchConstraintPattern in T1) strips message flags (-m, --title, etc.) but does not handle heredoc or generic quoted string content. This is a known limitation shared with built-in constraint patterns. A cat command with heredoc containing 'install' text can still trigger a package-manager false positive.

Live G8 subcommand-class false positive: rule-no-new-artifact-types regex matched the word "create" in "ck plan create --help", blocking the canonical plan-scaffolding command. T1 of 260602-meta-state-lifecycle-tidy (commit 1301ac2) wired splitSegments + stripMessageFlags into applyPromotedRules, which fixes the commit-message class (e.g. "git commit -m 'create new schema'") but not the subcommand-name class. The active rule pattern is "propose|design|create|new\s+(schema|artifact|directory|convention)" — bare "create" matches subcommand names like "plan create", "record_create_*", "meta_state_promote_rule" (which itself calls updateEntry). Fix requires either: (a) make the regex require a context qualifier (e.g. "create (a|an)?\s+(new|separate) (schema|artifact|...)"), or (b) maintain a subcommand-name allowlist in applyPromotedRules. Mitigation used in this plan: AGENTS.md-documented fallback (Create tool directly) — recorded here so the gap is visible to future plans.

SUPERSEDED 2026-06-06 by change-log meta-260606T0028Z-g8-subcommand-class-false-positive-supersede: bug is empirically NOT fixed by mechanism; the AGENTS.md 'use Create tool directly' workaround remains the active mitigation. The actual fix (regex qualifier or subcommand-name allowlist) is a separate plan.

Agent inside a project that has its own .mcp.json called ck:use-mcp — a cross-project tool-discovery skill. The skill's scripts were not installed; the install was blocked by the bash gate. The original entry was resolved as 'tool missing' with no persistent rule. Nothing prevents recurrence. Projects with their own learning-loop-mcp server should use mcp__learning_loop_mcp__* tools directly, not invoke cross-project discovery skills.

RESOLVED 2026-06-05: rule-project-skill-boundary promoted 2026-06-02T04:16:38Z. The rule (gate-enforced, glob '.factory/skills/{use-mcp,find-skills}/**', scope_predicate 'project_has_learning_loop_mcp') is the resolution. Also: schema patch affected_system agent-prompt -> mcp-tools applied.

Third documented G8 subcommand-class recurrence: rule-no-new-artifact-types regex matched the word 'create' in 'ck plan create --title ... --phases ... --dir 260602-sp0-log-change' (invoked during the SP0 plan scaffolding in plans/260602-sp0-log-change/). T1 of 260602-meta-state-lifecycle-tidy wired splitSegments + stripMessageFlags into applyPromotedRules, which fixes the commit-message class (e.g., 'git commit -m create new schema') but not the subcommand-name class. The pattern 'propose|design|create|new\s+(schema|artifact|directory|convention)' still matches bare 'create' in any subcommand. Mitigation per AGENTS.md: use the Create tool directly to scaffold plan files; this plan does that. The actual fix (regex qualifier or subcommand-name allowlist) is out of scope for the 3 related meta plans and remains an open follow-up.

SUPERSEDED 2026-06-06 by change-log meta-260606T0028Z-g8-subcommand-class-false-positive-supersede: bug is empirically NOT fixed by mechanism; the AGENTS.md 'use Create tool directly' workaround remains the active mitigation. The actual fix (regex qualifier or subcommand-name allowlist) is a separate plan.

Fourth documented G8 recurrence AND a partial regression of T1 (260602-meta-state-lifecycle-tidy): the rule-no-new-artifact-types regex matched banned words ('design', 'create', and the substring 'entry_kind' which contains the trigger) inside the body of a 'git commit -m ...' command, blocking the SP0 plan-artifact commit. T1 wired splitSegments + stripMessageFlags into applyPromotedRules to fix the commit-message class (e.g., 'git commit -m create new schema') but the T1 fix did NOT apply in this case — the gate matched the body content and blocked the commit. The banned words were: 'design' (in 'SP0 self-modification affordance spec + parent doc + red-team'), 'create' (in 'create files' / 'creating the report'), and the literal substring 'entry_kind' (which contains the trigger 'create' in the middle of a path-like token). Mitigation used: rewrote the commit message to avoid all banned words and used 'git commit -F /tmp/msg.txt' to bypass the inline-message class entirely. Both subsequent commits succeeded (72d8bb0 reports; 0d37ad0 plans). The T1 fix is incomplete: it strips the -m '...' value (the message) but does not protect against the rule's regex matching legitimate technical terms that happen to contain the trigger substring (e.g., 'entry_kind' contains 'create'). Fix requires either: (a) the stripMessageFlags function strips all flag values including the message body, not just the flag name, OR (b) the regex uses word boundaries to avoid matching 'create' inside longer tokens like 'entry_kind' or 'meta_state_create' or 'CREATE TABLE'.

SUPERSEDED 2026-06-06 by change-log meta-260606T0028Z-g8-subcommand-class-false-positive-supersede: bug is empirically NOT fixed by mechanism; the AGENTS.md 'use Create tool directly' workaround remains the active mitigation. The actual fix (regex qualifier or subcommand-name allowlist) is a separate plan.

G8 subcommand-class false positive (5th recurrence) hit ck plan create invocation for SP2 plan scaffolding. The pattern blocked the command despite operator intent being a legitimate plan scaffold. Workaround: scaffold plan files via the Create tool directly. Same as SP0 (4 prior recurrences).

SUPERSEDED 2026-06-06 by change-log meta-260606T0028Z-g8-subcommand-class-false-positive-supersede: bug is empirically NOT fixed by mechanism; the AGENTS.md 'use Create tool directly' workaround remains the active mitigation. The actual fix (regex qualifier or subcommand-name allowlist) is a separate plan.

loop_describe cold tier does not surface the lineage of superseded entries. The future 'superseded' status + consolidated_into field (captured in meta-260605T1330Z-g8-subcommand-class-false-positive-consolidation stub) will create an audit trail from old findings to their canonical change-log. loop_describe should expose this lineage so agents can trace old entries to the canonical source without reading the registry directly. Without this surface, the gap between 'drift events' (current state) and 'resolution events' (audit trail) widens: agents see the current state but not the history. Out of scope for the immediate Option A plan; captured here as a follow-up so it doesn't get lost.

The 5 new SP0-SP3 tools (meta_state_log_change, meta_state_derive_status, meta_state_check_grounding, meta_state_refresh_fingerprint, meta_state_query_drift) are only reachable when the MCP server is connected to the agent's session. In a fresh session, a new surface, or any session where the gate init did not trigger MCP client connection, the agent falls back to direct file I/O via Node scripts, bypassing the canonical entry points. This session demonstrated this: the meta-state cleanup (revert of G8 entries + drift analysis) had to use a Node script that imports core/meta-state.js, losing the appendGateLog audit trail and the canonical input validation the MCP tools provide. The 'use the loop's own machinery' promise depends on the MCP server being live. A SessionStart hook that re-validates the MCP connection and falls back gracefully would close this gap. Captured here as a follow-up.

MCP server probe failed at session start (reason=timeout, session_id=droid-debug-probe-2026-06-06). The 5 SP0-SP3 tools (meta_state_log_change, meta_state_derive_status, meta_state_check_grounding, meta_state_refresh_fingerprint, meta_state_query_drift) may be unreachable in this session. Workarounds: (1) try mcp__learning_loop_mcp__* tools directly (the probe may have failed transiently); (2) reconnect via session config; (3) fall back to direct file I/O via Node scripts that import core/meta-state.js.

loop-surface-inject.cjs#spawnAndCall has a chicken-and-egg deadlock: the initialize JSON-RPC message is written inside the child.stdout.on("data") handler (line 222), but the first stdout data from a freshly spawned MCP server is the response to initialize — which can never be sent because the data handler is what triggers the send. Result: every session-start probe hits the 10-second timeout, Phase 4 of plan 260605 logs a meta_state_report finding with reason=timeout, and the operator sees the MCP-failure banner. Verified empirically: 2026-06-06 by running the hook with realistic stdin ({"hook_event_name":"SessionStart","source":"startup"...}); banner printed with reason=timeout, finding logged as meta-260606T0142Z-mcp-connection-missing. The server itself is fine — a manual probe that waits for the stderr "MCP server started" line before sending initialize completes the handshake successfully (49/49 tools registered, loop_describe summary returned). The fix is to write initialize immediately after spawn (e.g., setTimeout 200ms) and remove the if (!initialized) block from inside the stdout data handler. Tests cover the mock-spawn path but never exercised the real spawnAndCall, so the bug slipped through. Blast radius: SessionStart hook only (verified via grep: spawnAndCall is only called in loop-surface-inject.cjs).

splitSegments in core/gate-logic.js:60-63 splits a bash command on ';' '&' '|' without tracking quote state (' " \). A quoted message body (e.g., git commit -m "body with ; inside; ... trigger words; ...") is fragmented BEFORE stripMessageFlags can handle it. Each fragment is then checked by applyPromotedRules independently; fragments after the ';' contain tokens that match active promoted rules (e.g., 'create a new schema' matches rule-no-new-artifact-types). Empirical proof 2026-06-06: a multi-paragraph git commit message describing the G8 fix (with legitimate mention of 'propose/design/create a new schema, new schema, create schema' as legitimate triggers) was escalated by the rule even though every trigger phrase was inside -m "..." quoted bodies. The P1 G8 subcommand-class fix (status=resolved, refined pattern) was therefore incomplete: it fixed the subcommand-name class (the 7 documented recurrences) but exposed this latent message-body-fragmentation class. Fix: rewrite splitSegments as a quote-aware state machine that tracks ', ", and \ escapes. Scope: every applyPromotedRules + matchConstraintPattern caller. Captured here BEFORE the fix per the operator's instruction (don't over-confidence; record then fix).
all_findings.evidence_journal: docs/journals/260606-g8-subcommand-class-fix.md (the P1 journal, which must be amended to acknowledge the deeper bug)

all_findings.description: Instruction layer for agents: should we add a loop_get_instruction MCP tool, extend loop_describe, or embed the rules in AGENTS.md? TBD in a future session. Position D's discoverability_hints is the at-start-up surface; the on-demand lookup is a separate concern. Skill (learning-loop) stays as prompt-author docs; agents need a separate, complementary surface. Captured from brainstorm thread at plans/reports/brainstorm-260606-discoverability-and-meta-evidence-migration.md.

MCP server is reachable (SessionStart hook probe succeeds — 49 tools, 8 record types, 2 active rules, 3 active findings) but the agent's callable tool list does not include mcp__learning_loop_mcp__* tools. This is a client-side layer issue distinct from the server-side probe failure closed by the spawnAndCall fix (meta-260606T0200Z-loop-surface-inject-spawnandcall-chicken-egg-fix). Layer 1 (server-side probe) is WORKING. Layer 2 (client-side tool loading) is NOT WORKING in this session. Repro: 2026-06-06 by running `echo '{"hook_event_name":"SessionStart","source":"startup"}' | node .factory/hooks/loop-surface-inject.cjs` — returns the success block, but the agent's tool surface still lacks MCP tools. Likely cause: Droid/Factory agent runtime did not read .mcp.json + connect to the MCP server before populating the agent's tool list, OR the runtime is intentionally not exposing MCP tools to the brainstorming Skill context. The instruction-layer meta-state entry (meta-260606T0421Z-instruction-layer-for-agents-tbd) is a related but distinct question (where the operating manual lives) — this finding is specifically about MCP tool availability.
all_findings.resolution: Added .factory/mcp.json (Droid reads project-level MCP config from .factory/, not .mcp.json at project root). Cold-session test was checking for canonical MCP name format mcp__learning_loop_mcp__ which Droid never emits; updated detection to 'learning-loop-mcp' which matches Droid's actual --list-tools output. Cold-session test now passes; mcp-client-loading-missing finding soft-deleted by test. Rule rule-cold-session-test-must-pass-before-resolution satisfied.

all_findings.description: Closeout script (scripts/closeout-meta-evidence-migration.cjs) was not idempotent for the change-log append step. The script checked status for finding resolution (correctly idempotent) but unconditionally called writeEntry() for the change-log, generating a new timestamp-based ID every run. Result: two identical change-log entries (meta-260606T1433Z and meta-260606T1453Z) were created before the bug was discovered during code review. Fix: added a find() guard that checks for existing entry with same entry_kind + change_target + consolidates before calling writeEntry(). Cleanup script removed the duplicate. Pattern: all future direct-I/O scripts that append entries must implement idempotency guards (either deduplication check before write, or deterministic ID generation).

droid exec --list-tools does not expose mcp__learning_loop_mcp__* tools in this environment. The MCP server is reachable (server-side probe works — see meta-260606T0200Z-loop-surface-inject-spawnandcall-chicken-egg-fix), but the droid agent runtime is not loading project-local MCP servers into its tool list. This is the client-side gap described in meta-260606T0443Z-mcp-tools-not-loaded-into-agent-tool-list. Detected by cold-session-discoverability.test.cjs#droid exec exposes mcp__learning_loop_mcp__* tools.

Related finding (target of this rule): meta-260606T0443Z-mcp-tools-not-loaded-into-agent-tool-list

Design captured for a future plan: rule-cold-session-test-must-pass-before-resolution. Intent: promote cold-session-discoverability.test.cjs (the droid exec exposes mcp__learning_loop_mcp__* tools test) as a gate-enforced rule that gates the resolution of meta-260606T0443Z-mcp-tools-not-loaded-into-agent-tool-list. Design (deferred 2026-06-06 per operator decision): new pattern_type resolution-evidence-required in gate-logic.js#applyPromotedRules; new checkResolutionEvidence(rule, root) helper that reads meta-state.jsonl and asserts no active finding with subtype=mcp-client-loading and session_id=test-cold-session-mcp-client-loading; meta_state_resolve consults the rule before updateEntry and returns { resolved: false, reason: resolution_evidence_required } if the check fails. Test update required: the cold-session test must also DELETE its finding when the gap closes (currently it only logs on gap-open; the gate would block resolution on stale findings otherwise - the existing 24h TTL would outlast the actual state of the gap). Related artifacts: the test was added 2026-06-06 (cold-session-discoverability.test.cjs); the new pattern_type is a strict superset of regex/glob in semantic intent (it is not a command-path match); the test idempotency guard is keyed on session_id, which is the same key the rule check uses. This entry is the design note; it is NOT promoted_to_rule (enforcement is not yet gate). Operators and future plans discover it via loop_describe warm tier (active findings) or meta_state_list with subtype=cold-session-test-rule-design.
all_findings.resolution: Plan 260606-cold-session-test-rule-promotion shipped: rule mechanism in core/gate-logic.js#checkResolutionEvidence; consultation in meta_state_resolve; cold-session test extended to soft-delete on gap-close.

all_findings.description: Schema enhancement design captured for a future plan: add formal cross-reference field for this change-log/finding targets or relates to that finding relationships. Current state (2026-06-06): meta-state.jsonl has no formal related-to or design-target field. The only formal cross-reference fields are (1) consolidated_into (finding -> change-log; valid only with status=superseded), (2) consolidates (change-log -> finding; means these findings are superseded by this change-log), and (3) supersedes (change-log -> change-log). None of these express this design note targets that active finding. Proposed shape (option A): extend change-log applies_to to include findings: applies_to.findings: z.array(z.string()).optional(). Proposed shape (option B): add a generic related_to: z.array(z.string()).optional() to both finding and change-log schemas. Use case: design notes (like the cold-session test rule design captured in meta-260606T1531Z-cold-session-test-rule-deferred, which targets meta-260606T0443Z-mcp-tools-not-loaded-into-agent-tool-list) need to formally reference the target finding they are a design for. Workaround in use today: mention the target finding id in the description field (discoverable via grep, not via schema). Status: design note; deferred for a future plan per operator decision 2026-06-06. Operators and future plans discover this design note via meta_state_list with subtype=meta-state-schema-enhancement.
all_findings.resolution: Superseded by entry_kind: rule | loop-design first-class schema (4-kind union). Cross-references are typed fields on the new schemas (proposed_design_for, addresses, origin). The original finding was the operator's first attempt at expressing "this design targets that finding" via a generic related_to field; the 4-kind union is the proper expression.

all_findings.description: Gate-enforced rule: meta_state_resolve cannot resolve meta-260606T0443Z-mcp-tools-not-loaded-into-agent-tool-list unless the cold-session test (cold-session-discoverability.test.cjs#droid exec exposes mcp__learning_loop_mcp__* tools) has been run and its most recent evidence shows the gap is closed (no active finding with subtype=mcp-client-loading and session_id=test-cold-session-mcp-client-loading). The check is in checkResolutionEvidence in core/gate-logic.js. This rule design is captured in meta-260606T1531Z-cold-session-test-rule-deferred.

Context pollution: stale workaround language + audit-trail noise bias fresh readers toward "G8 is still broken." The G8 subcommand-class + message-body-fragmentation classes are BOTH fixed (P1 refined regex at meta-260606T0225Z + P2 quote-aware splitSegments at the change-log resolving meta-260606T0301Z, status=resolved). Empirical probe 2026-06-06 confirms: (a) `git commit -m "propose/design/create a new schema pattern"` returns decision=ok (P2); (b) `ck plan create --title foo --phases bar` returns decision=ok (P1); (c) legitimate triggers like `echo propose/design/create a new schema` still escalate (rule still enforcing correctly).

Three pollution sources:

1. AGENTS.md "use Create tool directly" + "git commit -F /tmp/msg.txt" workarounds predate both P1 and P2. They are now stale (proven by this probe) but still steer fresh agents away from canonical paths. A fresh agent reading AGENTS.md will use -F for safety, masking any future P1/P2 regression instead of catching it. Mitigation: replace the workaround paragraphs with a "verified working" note pointing at the regression probes.

2. Meta-state.jsonl audit trail: 4 superseded G8 finding entries (recurrences 1, 3, 4, 5), 3 change-logs, the meta-260606T0301Z finding, and the active rule entry combine into ~9 entries that all narrate the G8 fix story. The entries are correctly linked (consolidated_into, supersedes), but a fresh reader skimming meta_state_list will see a wall of "G8" and bias toward "G8 is open." Mitigation: out of scope for this finding — the audit trail is by design.

3. Self-applied workaround during mcp fix: I used `git commit -F /tmp/commit-msg-N.txt` for commits 1692b84 and a35074a. The probe shows this was unnecessary — inline `-m "..."` with trigger phrases would have passed. Applying the old workaround to a fixed bug is itself the pollution: future agents following my commit history will copy the -F pattern without questioning it. Mitigation: the next commit can use inline -m to reset the example, but no retroactive fix is needed.

Recommended follow-up (separate work):
- Update AGENTS.md § "G8 Subcommand Class" + "Create tool fallback" sections to mark them as historical workarounds superseded by P1+P2.
- Add a permanent regression probe in `tools/learning-loop-mcp/__tests__/` that runs the 5-case G8 matrix on every change to `core/gate-logic.js` or `promoted_to_rule.pattern` (locking in the fix so future regressions are caught).

Agent used direct file I/O (node -e scripts importing core/meta-state.js) for meta-state CRUD operations instead of the canonical MCP tools (meta_state_resolve, meta_state_log_change, meta_state_report, etc.). During Phase 0 of plan 260606-rule-loop-design-first-class, the agent bypassed the MCP tool layer for registry mutations. This is a procedural anti-pattern: the MCP tools provide validation, audit logging, and canonical semantics that direct I/O lacks.

Agent called meta_state_log_change MCP tool 5 times in succession due to apparent failure/uncertainty, each time appending a new change-log entry with identical change_target + reason but different timestamps. This created 5 duplicate entries (meta-260606T2059Z) in meta-state.jsonl before the agent realized the issue and cleaned them up. Root cause: the tool lacks a deduplication guard before writeEntry, and the agent's retry loop did not check for existing entries before re-invoking. Same pattern as meta-260606T2059Z-meta-state-jsonl closeout finding (which documented the same gap in a different script). The cleanup required manual Edit-tool deletion of 4 duplicate lines from meta-state.jsonl. This is the second recurrence of the duplicate-entry gap in the same session, proving the fix (manual cleanup) is not sustainable. A deterministic idempotency guard in meta_state_log_change or an agent-side dedup check before re-invocation is needed.

Deferred design (concrete adoption of loop-design-instruction-layer parent): add 7th hint to core/loop-introspect.js#DISCOVERABILITY_HINTS that teaches the agent the meta-vs-product-vs-in-flight surface split before answering "what's next?" style questions. Hint text: "For 'what's next?' questions, distinguish surfaces first: meta work lives in meta_state_list({ status: 'reported' }); product work lives in index_search on records/**/plans/; in-flight work lives in plans/<id>/phase-*.md. Don't query meta-state for product tasks." Trade-off decision: teaching hint over forced query (avoids prompt-attention competition + brittleness); teaching hint over new workflow_next_task tool (KISS + YAGNI — workflow_intake_orient already orients). Surface: core/loop-introspect.js (1 line in frozen array). Validation: cold-session test (rule-cold-session-test-must-pass-before-resolution) — agent asked "what's next?" must call BOTH meta_state_list AND index_search and tell the operator the surface split. Impact: 0 new schema, 0 new tool, ~50 tokens/agent-session. Status: deferred pending operator approval of parent loop-design-instruction-layer adoption. NOT shipped yet — this entry records the design decision so future agents see it in the registry without it being a done deal.

Top-level array and boolean parameters in MCP tool schemas get coerced to objects/strings on the wire, causing zod validation to reject valid input. Reproduced 2026-06-06 on meta_state_propose_design: passing proposed_design_for=['x'] or addresses=['y'] produces error 'expected array, received object' at path 'proposed_design_for'. Same pattern on meta_state_report: passing mechanism_check=false (boolean) produces 'expected boolean, received string'. Workaround used: drop the boolean field and accept partial registration. Root cause hypothesis: the MCP server's parameter parsing treats a single-element array as if the first element is an object key. Compare to meta_state_log_change which only takes arrays nested inside object fields (change_diff.added, applies_to.tools) — those work because the object wrapper absorbs the coercion. This is a different fault class from meta-260606T2106Z-agent-called-meta-state-log-change-mcp-too (which was idempotency, not wire format). Impact: agents cannot use meta_state_propose_design cleanly and fall back to meta_state_report (finding kind) for designs that should be loop-design kind, polluting the registry with the wrong entry_kind.

Dual-field schema risk: `evidence_code_ref` (top-level) vs `evidence.code_ref` (nested) causes active bug in `queryDrift` that skips SP2 grounding for 30 entries. The `finding` schema defines `evidence_code_ref` as top-level, but 30 entries in the registry carry only the nested `evidence.code_ref` form. The `change-log` schema defines `evidence.code_ref` as nested. Consumers `checkGrounding`, `deriveStatus`, and `refreshFingerprint` use a fallback chain `entry.evidence_code_ref ?? entry.evidence?.code_ref`, but `queryDrift` only checks `entry.evidence_code_ref` — skipping 30 entries from drift detection. The `meta-state-report-tool` writes both fields for backward compatibility; `meta-state-log-change-tool` writes only the nested form. No migration or normalization logic exists. `writeEntry` does not validate against Zod schemas, so invalid data shapes are accepted silently.

anti_patterns.description:
Live G8 subcommand-class false positive: rule-no-new-artifact-types regex matched the word "create" in "ck plan create --help", blocking the canonical plan-scaffolding command. T1 of 260602-meta-state-lifecycle-tidy (commit 1301ac2) wired splitSegments + stripMessageFlags into applyPromotedRules, which fixes the commit-message class (e.g. "git commit -m 'create new schema'") but not the subcommand-name class. The active rule pattern is "propose|design|create|new\s+(schema|artifact|directory|convention)" — bare "create" matches subcommand names like "plan create", "record_create_*", "meta_state_promote_rule" (which itself calls updateEntry). Fix requires either: (a) make the regex require a context qualifier (e.g. "create (a|an)?\s+(new|separate) (schema|artifact|...)"), or (b) maintain a subcommand-name allowlist in applyPromotedRules. Mitigation used in this plan: AGENTS.md-documented fallback (Create tool directly) — recorded here so the gap is visible to future plans.

SUPERSEDED 2026-06-06 by change-log meta-260606T0028Z-g8-subcommand-class-false-positive-supersede: bug is empirically NOT fixed by mechanism; the AGENTS.md 'use Create tool directly' workaround remains the active mitigation. The actual fix (regex qualifier or subcommand-name allowlist) is a separate plan.

Third documented G8 subcommand-class recurrence: rule-no-new-artifact-types regex matched the word 'create' in 'ck plan create --title ... --phases ... --dir 260602-sp0-log-change' (invoked during the SP0 plan scaffolding in plans/260602-sp0-log-change/). T1 of 260602-meta-state-lifecycle-tidy wired splitSegments + stripMessageFlags into applyPromotedRules, which fixes the commit-message class (e.g., 'git commit -m create new schema') but not the subcommand-name class. The pattern 'propose|design|create|new\s+(schema|artifact|directory|convention)' still matches bare 'create' in any subcommand. Mitigation per AGENTS.md: use the Create tool directly to scaffold plan files; this plan does that. The actual fix (regex qualifier or subcommand-name allowlist) is out of scope for the 3 related meta plans and remains an open follow-up.

SUPERSEDED 2026-06-06 by change-log meta-260606T0028Z-g8-subcommand-class-false-positive-supersede: bug is empirically NOT fixed by mechanism; the AGENTS.md 'use Create tool directly' workaround remains the active mitigation. The actual fix (regex qualifier or subcommand-name allowlist) is a separate plan.

Fourth documented G8 recurrence AND a partial regression of T1 (260602-meta-state-lifecycle-tidy): the rule-no-new-artifact-types regex matched banned words ('design', 'create', and the substring 'entry_kind' which contains the trigger) inside the body of a 'git commit -m ...' command, blocking the SP0 plan-artifact commit. T1 wired splitSegments + stripMessageFlags into applyPromotedRules to fix the commit-message class (e.g., 'git commit -m create new schema') but the T1 fix did NOT apply in this case — the gate matched the body content and blocked the commit. The banned words were: 'design' (in 'SP0 self-modification affordance spec + parent doc + red-team'), 'create' (in 'create files' / 'creating the report'), and the literal substring 'entry_kind' (which contains the trigger 'create' in the middle of a path-like token). Mitigation used: rewrote the commit message to avoid all banned words and used 'git commit -F /tmp/msg.txt' to bypass the inline-message class entirely. Both subsequent commits succeeded (72d8bb0 reports; 0d37ad0 plans). The T1 fix is incomplete: it strips the -m '...' value (the message) but does not protect against the rule's regex matching legitimate technical terms that happen to contain the trigger substring (e.g., 'entry_kind' contains 'create'). Fix requires either: (a) the stripMessageFlags function strips all flag values including the message body, not just the flag name, OR (b) the regex uses word boundaries to avoid matching 'create' inside longer tokens like 'entry_kind' or 'meta_state_create' or 'CREATE TABLE'.

SUPERSEDED 2026-06-06 by change-log meta-260606T0028Z-g8-subcommand-class-false-positive-supersede: bug is empirically NOT fixed by mechanism; the AGENTS.md 'use Create tool directly' workaround remains the active mitigation. The actual fix (regex qualifier or subcommand-name allowlist) is a separate plan.

Instruction layer for agents: should we add a loop_get_instruction MCP tool, extend loop_describe, or embed the rules in AGENTS.md? TBD in a future session. Position D's discoverability_hints is the at-start-up surface; the on-demand lookup is a separate concern. Skill (learning-loop) stays as prompt-author docs; agents need a separate, complementary surface. Captured from brainstorm thread at plans/reports/brainstorm-260606-discoverability-and-meta-evidence-migration.md.

Closeout script (scripts/closeout-meta-evidence-migration.cjs) was not idempotent for the change-log append step. The script checked status for finding resolution (correctly idempotent) but unconditionally called writeEntry() for the change-log, generating a new timestamp-based ID every run. Result: two identical change-log entries (meta-260606T1433Z and meta-260606T1453Z) were created before the bug was discovered during code review. Fix: added a find() guard that checks for existing entry with same entry_kind + change_target + consolidates before calling writeEntry(). Cleanup script removed the duplicate. Pattern: all future direct-I/O scripts that append entries must implement idempotency guards (either deduplication check before write, or deterministic ID generation).

Gate-enforced rule: meta_state_resolve cannot resolve meta-260606T0443Z-mcp-tools-not-loaded-into-agent-tool-list unless the cold-session test (cold-session-discoverability.test.cjs#droid exec exposes mcp__learning_loop_mcp__* tools) has been run and its most recent evidence shows the gap is closed (no active finding with subtype=mcp-client-loading and session_id=test-cold-session-mcp-client-loading). The check is in checkResolutionEvidence in core/gate-logic.js. This rule design is captured in meta-260606T1531Z-cold-session-test-rule-deferred.

Context pollution: stale workaround language + audit-trail noise bias fresh readers toward "G8 is still broken." The G8 subcommand-class + message-body-fragmentation classes are BOTH fixed (P1 refined regex at meta-260606T0225Z + P2 quote-aware splitSegments at the change-log resolving meta-260606T0301Z, status=resolved). Empirical probe 2026-06-06 confirms: (a) `git commit -m "propose/design/create a new schema pattern"` returns decision=ok (P2); (b) `ck plan create --title foo --phases bar` returns decision=ok (P1); (c) legitimate triggers like `echo propose/design/create a new schema` still escalate (rule still enforcing correctly).

Three pollution sources:

1. AGENTS.md "use Create tool directly" + "git commit -F /tmp/msg.txt" workarounds predate both P1 and P2. They are now stale (proven by this probe) but still steer fresh agents away from canonical paths. A fresh agent reading AGENTS.md will use -F for safety, masking any future P1/P2 regression instead of catching it. Mitigation: replace the workaround paragraphs with a "verified working" note pointing at the regression probes.

2. Meta-state.jsonl audit trail: 4 superseded G8 finding entries (recurrences 1, 3, 4, 5), 3 change-logs, the meta-260606T0301Z finding, and the active rule entry combine into ~9 entries that all narrate the G8 fix story. The entries are correctly linked (consolidated_into, supersedes), but a fresh reader skimming meta_state_list will see a wall of "G8" and bias toward "G8 is open." Mitigation: out of scope for this finding — the audit trail is by design.

3. Self-applied workaround during mcp fix: I used `git commit -F /tmp/commit-msg-N.txt` for commits 1692b84 and a35074a. The probe shows this was unnecessary — inline `-m "..."` with trigger phrases would have passed. Applying the old workaround to a fixed bug is itself the pollution: future agents following my commit history will copy the -F pattern without questioning it. Mitigation: the next commit can use inline -m to reset the example, but no retroactive fix is needed.

Recommended follow-up (separate work):
- Update AGENTS.md § "G8 Subcommand Class" + "Create tool fallback" sections to mark them as historical workarounds superseded by P1+P2.
- Add a permanent regression probe in `tools/learning-loop-mcp/__tests__/` that runs the 5-case G8 matrix on every change to `core/gate-logic.js` or `promoted_to_rule.pattern` (locking in the fix so future regressions are caught).

Agent used direct file I/O (node -e scripts importing core/meta-state.js) for meta-state CRUD operations instead of the canonical MCP tools (meta_state_resolve, meta_state_log_change, meta_state_report, etc.). During Phase 0 of plan 260606-rule-loop-design-first-class, the agent bypassed the MCP tool layer for registry mutations. This is a procedural anti-pattern: the MCP tools provide validation, audit logging, and canonical semantics that direct I/O lacks.

Agent called meta_state_log_change MCP tool 5 times in succession due to apparent failure/uncertainty, each time appending a new change-log entry with identical change_target + reason but different timestamps. This created 5 duplicate entries (meta-260606T2059Z) in meta-state.jsonl before the agent realized the issue and cleaned them up. Root cause: the tool lacks a deduplication guard before writeEntry, and the agent's retry loop did not check for existing entries before re-invoking. Same pattern as meta-260606T2059Z-meta-state-jsonl closeout finding (which documented the same gap in a different script). The cleanup required manual Edit-tool deletion of 4 duplicate lines from meta-state.jsonl. This is the second recurrence of the duplicate-entry gap in the same session, proving the fix (manual cleanup) is not sustainable. A deterministic idempotency guard in meta_state_log_change or an agent-side dedup check before re-invocation is needed.

Deferred design (concrete adoption of loop-design-instruction-layer parent): add 7th hint to core/loop-introspect.js#DISCOVERABILITY_HINTS that teaches the agent the meta-vs-product-vs-in-flight surface split before answering "what's next?" style questions. Hint text: "For 'what's next?' questions, distinguish surfaces first: meta work lives in meta_state_list({ status: 'reported' }); product work lives in index_search on records/**/plans/; in-flight work lives in plans/<id>/phase-*.md. Don't query meta-state for product tasks." Trade-off decision: teaching hint over forced query (avoids prompt-attention competition + brittleness); teaching hint over new workflow_next_task tool (KISS + YAGNI — workflow_intake_orient already orients). Surface: core/loop-introspect.js (1 line in frozen array). Validation: cold-session test (rule-cold-session-test-must-pass-before-resolution) — agent asked "what's next?" must call BOTH meta_state_list AND index_search and tell the operator the surface split. Impact: 0 new schema, 0 new tool, ~50 tokens/agent-session. Status: deferred pending operator approval of parent loop-design-instruction-layer adoption. NOT shipped yet — this entry records the design decision so future agents see it in the registry without it being a done deal.

Top-level array and boolean parameters in MCP tool schemas get coerced to objects/strings on the wire, causing zod validation to reject valid input. Reproduced 2026-06-06 on meta_state_propose_design: passing proposed_design_for=['x'] or addresses=['y'] produces error 'expected array, received object' at path 'proposed_design_for'. Same pattern on meta_state_report: passing mechanism_check=false (boolean) produces 'expected boolean, received string'. Workaround used: drop the boolean field and accept partial registration. Root cause hypothesis: the MCP server's parameter parsing treats a single-element array as if the first element is an object key. Compare to meta_state_log_change which only takes arrays nested inside object fields (change_diff.added, applies_to.tools) — those work because the object wrapper absorbs the coercion. This is a different fault class from meta-260606T2106Z-agent-called-meta-state-log-change-mcp-too (which was idempotency, not wire format). Impact: agents cannot use meta_state_propose_design cleanly and fall back to meta_state_report (finding kind) for designs that should be loop-design kind, polluting the registry with the wrong entry_kind.

loop_designs.title: Instruction layer for agents — on-demand rule lookup
Meta-state cross-reference fields — typed fields on rule and loop-design schemas

change_log.reason: Consolidate 4 G8 subcommand-class false-positive finding entries (recurrences 1, 3, 4, 5) into a single change-log. Empirical test 2026-06-06 confirmed: bug is NOT fixed by mechanism. applyPromotedRules still matches bare 'create' in subcommand names like 'ck plan create' and 'meta_state_promote_rule'. The commit-message class was fixed by T1 of 260602-meta-state-lifecycle-tidy (stripMessageFlags), but the subcommand-name class requires either a regex qualifier or a subcommand-name allowlist — captured here as a follow-up. The 4 stale finding entries are now 'superseded' with consolidated_into: <this id>. The 6th recurrence (Phase 0 SP3 plan scaffolding) and 7th recurrence (Phase 0 of this plan) are captured in separate change-log entries.

findings.description:
Live G8 subcommand-class false positive: rule-no-new-artifact-types regex matched the word "create" in "ck plan create --help", blocking the canonical plan-scaffolding command. T1 of 260602-meta-state-lifecycle-tidy (commit 1301ac2) wired splitSegments + stripMessageFlags into applyPromotedRules, which fixes the commit-message class (e.g. "git commit -m 'create new schema'") but not the subcommand-name class. The active rule pattern is "propose|design|create|new\s+(schema|artifact|directory|convention)" — bare "create" matches subcommand names like "plan create", "record_create_*", "meta_state_promote_rule" (which itself calls updateEntry). Fix requires either: (a) make the regex require a context qualifier (e.g. "create (a|an)?\s+(new|separate) (schema|artifact|...)"), or (b) maintain a subcommand-name allowlist in applyPromotedRules. Mitigation used in this plan: AGENTS.md-documented fallback (Create tool directly) — recorded here so the gap is visible to future plans.

SUPERSEDED 2026-06-06 by change-log meta-260606T0028Z-g8-subcommand-class-false-positive-supersede: bug is empirically NOT fixed by mechanism; the AGENTS.md 'use Create tool directly' workaround remains the active mitigation. The actual fix (regex qualifier or subcommand-name allowlist) is a separate plan.

Fourth documented G8 recurrence AND a partial regression of T1 (260602-meta-state-lifecycle-tidy): the rule-no-new-artifact-types regex matched banned words ('design', 'create', and the substring 'entry_kind' which contains the trigger) inside the body of a 'git commit -m ...' command, blocking the SP0 plan-artifact commit. T1 wired splitSegments + stripMessageFlags into applyPromotedRules to fix the commit-message class (e.g., 'git commit -m create new schema') but the T1 fix did NOT apply in this case — the gate matched the body content and blocked the commit. The banned words were: 'design' (in 'SP0 self-modification affordance spec + parent doc + red-team'), 'create' (in 'create files' / 'creating the report'), and the literal substring 'entry_kind' (which contains the trigger 'create' in the middle of a path-like token). Mitigation used: rewrote the commit message to avoid all banned words and used 'git commit -F /tmp/msg.txt' to bypass the inline-message class entirely. Both subsequent commits succeeded (72d8bb0 reports; 0d37ad0 plans). The T1 fix is incomplete: it strips the -m '...' value (the message) but does not protect against the rule's regex matching legitimate technical terms that happen to contain the trigger substring (e.g., 'entry_kind' contains 'create'). Fix requires either: (a) the stripMessageFlags function strips all flag values including the message body, not just the flag name, OR (b) the regex uses word boundaries to avoid matching 'create' inside longer tokens like 'entry_kind' or 'meta_state_create' or 'CREATE TABLE'.

SUPERSEDED 2026-06-06 by change-log meta-260606T0028Z-g8-subcommand-class-false-positive-supersede: bug is empirically NOT fixed by mechanism; the AGENTS.md 'use Create tool directly' workaround remains the active mitigation. The actual fix (regex qualifier or subcommand-name allowlist) is a separate plan.

Third documented G8 subcommand-class recurrence: rule-no-new-artifact-types regex matched the word 'create' in 'ck plan create --title ... --phases ... --dir 260602-sp0-log-change' (invoked during the SP0 plan scaffolding in plans/260602-sp0-log-change/). T1 of 260602-meta-state-lifecycle-tidy wired splitSegments + stripMessageFlags into applyPromotedRules, which fixes the commit-message class (e.g., 'git commit -m create new schema') but not the subcommand-name class. The pattern 'propose|design|create|new\s+(schema|artifact|directory|convention)' still matches bare 'create' in any subcommand. Mitigation per AGENTS.md: use the Create tool directly to scaffold plan files; this plan does that. The actual fix (regex qualifier or subcommand-name allowlist) is out of scope for the 3 related meta plans and remains an open follow-up.

SUPERSEDED 2026-06-06 by change-log meta-260606T0028Z-g8-subcommand-class-false-positive-supersede: bug is empirically NOT fixed by mechanism; the AGENTS.md 'use Create tool directly' workaround remains the active mitigation. The actual fix (regex qualifier or subcommand-name allowlist) is a separate plan.

G8 subcommand-class false positive (5th recurrence) hit ck plan create invocation for SP2 plan scaffolding. The pattern blocked the command despite operator intent being a legitimate plan scaffold. Workaround: scaffold plan files via the Create tool directly. Same as SP0 (4 prior recurrences).

SUPERSEDED 2026-06-06 by change-log meta-260606T0028Z-g8-subcommand-class-false-positive-supersede: bug is empirically NOT fixed by mechanism; the AGENTS.md 'use Create tool directly' workaround remains the active mitigation. The actual fix (regex qualifier or subcommand-name allowlist) is a separate plan.

discoverability_hints: To cite a thing, point at the code: `meta_state_report({ evidence_code_ref: 'path/to/file.js:line' })`. The loop will hash and re-check it.
For `source_refs`, prefer `local:meta-state:<id>` (cite a finding). Markdown refs (`local:plans/...`) are accepted for the escape hatch but discouraged.
Run `meta_state_derive_status({ id })` to re-check if a finding is still true. Run `meta_state_refresh_fingerprint({ id })` to re-hash the code after a refactor.
For designs without code, cite the change-log that records the design (`meta_state_log_change` with `change_target: '<plan-path>'`).
Findings have 5 statuses: `reported` (24h TTL), `active` (operator-acked), `resolved` (closed), `expired` (TTL elapsed), `superseded` (consolidated into a change-log).

For rule and loop-design lifecycle, use `meta_state_list({ entry_kind: 'rule' | 'loop-design' })` (Phase 3) or `loop_describe({ tier: 'cold' })` (Phase 4). The cold tier surfaces a `loop_designs` list with `id`, `title`, `proposed_design_for`, `addresses`, and `shipped_in_plan`.
